nodes.label: Aspect-Oriented Programming (AOP)
nodes.rationale: Modularizes cross-cutting functionality to reduce coupling and mitigate limitations that emerge in object-oriented systems.

nodes.label: Separation of Concerns
nodes.rationale: Separates common application-wide functionality from functionality specific to each module.

nodes.label: Minimal Extensible AOP Framework
nodes.rationale: A custom framework was preferred to large complex products to provide fine control, comfort, extensibility, and technology-independent aspect implementations.

nodes.label: Aspect Priority Ordering
nodes.rationale: Explicit priorities decouple aspect execution order from source attribute order and preserve architectural governance as aspects are added or removed.

nodes.label: Logging as an Aspect
nodes.rationale: An attribute hides standardized event messages, prevents accidental omission, and strengthens logging governance.

nodes.label: Microsoft Enterprise Library Logging Application Block
Exception Handling as an Aspect
nodes.rationale: Interception centralizes exception policies, improves governance, and prevents sensitive stack traces from reaching users.

nodes.label: Layered Exception Strategy
nodes.rationale: Propagate within a layer, wrap across layer boundaries, and replace at the UI boundary to avoid exposing sensitive information.

nodes.label: Dynamic Entity Configuration Loading
nodes.rationale: Reflection plus dynamic dispatch loads configuration classes automatically to reduce cyclomatic complexity and coupling in large models.

nodes.label: Embedded Resource Dataset
nodes.rationale: Stores large seed datasets outside source-code object initializers to avoid Visual Studio degradation.

nodes.label: Reflection over Entity Framework Internal APIs
nodes.rationale: Internal metadata is required for mappings unavailable through the public Entity Framework 5 API, at the cost of version fragility.

nodes.label: Degenerated Agile Build-and-Deploy Delivery
nodes.rationale: Unhealthy nonstop delivery pressure favors speed-focused developer behavior at the expense of polish and knowledge transfer.

nodes.label: Balanced Ninja-Zen Programmer
nodes.rationale: Organizations need both rapid problem solving and patient quality-oriented architecture, ideally combined in one balanced practitioner.

nodes.label: Reciprocal Importance of Others' Work
nodes.rationale: Treating other people's work as important creates a reciprocal culture in which they value yours.

nodes.label: Technical Code Audit
nodes.rationale: Combining compiled code metrics with physical line, blank, and documentation counts gives a fuller maintainability picture than LOC alone.

nodes.label: Developer-Centered Platform Design
nodes.rationale: .NET, C#, and Visual Studio were designed to feel natural to programmers and remove operational friction so they could focus on application quality.

nodes.label: Synchronized APIM and Stripe Product IDs
nodes.rationale: Matching product identifiers across APIM and Stripe connects measured consumption to the correct billable product.

nodes.label: Delegated Subscription Management
nodes.rationale: An external web application handles billing data and subscription workflows that the built-in Developer Portal cannot customize.

nodes.label: Cosmos DB Change Feed and Transactional Batches
